failure_surface: Agent executed code successfully but produced static cell values instead of Excel formulas, failing to implement the requested lookup logic as dynamic formulas. The evaluation returned 0 test cases passed despite successful code execution.
root_cause: The task required implementing lookup functionality via Excel formulas (VLOOKUP/MATCH style), but the agent computed values externally in Python and wrote them as static values to cells. This violates the implicit requirement for formula-based solutions when users request formula implementations that 'didn't work'. The SKILL.md lacks explicit guidance on distinguishing when static values vs. formulas should be written.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When a user reports lookup/formula attempts failed, treat the request as a formula implementation task. Write Excel formula strings via openpyxl.cell.value (e.g., '=VLOOKUP(...)') rather than computing results externally and writing static values.
evidence: User instruction: 'I have attempted to use VLOOKUP and MATCH functions without success' indicates they need working formulas, not pre-calculated values
Answer position specification 'C4:F6' defines target cells for formulas, not just values
Test case results [0] despite successful code execution ('Done' printed)
Solution code writes: ws.cell(row=row, column=col).value = matched[...] which assigns static Python values, not formula strings starting with '='
SKILL.md Section 4 states 'Never replace a formula string with a pre-calculated value unless explicitly instructed' - violation of this principle

memory_items.title: Formula Detection Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When users mention existing formula attempts (VLOOKUP, MATCH, INDEX, etc.) that failed, interpret this as a request for dynamic formulas, not external computation and static value insertion.
memory_items.generalization: Look for keywords like 'formula', 'function', 'VLOOKUP', 'MATCH', 'doesn't work' in cell manipulation requests. These signal formula implementation needs.

memory_items.title: Formula Writing Protocol
memory_items.lesson: To write formulas to cells via openpyxl, assign formula strings starting with '=' directly to cell.value, not pre-computed Python values.
memory_items.generalization: Use ws.cell(...).value = '=FORMULA_STRING()' pattern for dynamic results. Only compute externally when user explicitly asks for static values.

memory_items.title: Validation Before Formula Replacement
memory_items.lesson: Before replacing cell content with computed values, check if the cell originally contained or should contain formulas, especially when the task involves lookup/logic operations.
memory_items.generalization: Preserve formula references during manipulation. If task requires conditional logic that varies by row, write a single formula pattern that copies down rather than individual static values.

edits.content:
**Formula Request Recognition:** When users report formula attempts that failed (mentions of VLOOKUP, MATCH, INDEX, IF, etc.), prioritize writing actual Excel formulas over external computation. Assign formula strings directly to cell.value (e.g., `=VLOOKUP(A1,B:C,2,FALSE)`). Static value substitution only occurs when explicitly instructed.

**Cell Content Verification:** Before overwriting cells with computed values, verify the original intent. If task describes lookup, aggregation, or conditional logic across rows, a formula-based solution typically serves better than pre-calculated values.
